summary: No-ship: the plan breaks existing public interfaces, states a false traversal contract, and relies on a characterization lock that cannot detect a broken temporary-file convention.

findings.title: Simpler alternative: deepen existing modules behind compatibility facades
findings.body: The behavior-preserving claim conflicts with removing `pub mod path` and changing `KeyLocks::lock(&str)` to a two-argument method. Both are currently public, and Cargo.toml does not disable publication. An in-repository grep cannot prove that downstream consumers do not exist, while the 92-test lock contains no downstream compile test. Following the plan therefore creates source-incompatible behavior despite B10. A simpler refactoring is to deepen `path` in place—or retain it as a re-exporting compatibility facade—and add an internal `lock_for(bucket, key)` while preserving `lock(&str)`.
findings.recommendation: Preserve every existing public path and signature through compatibility wrappers, or reclassify the work as a breaking change with an explicit migration/versioning plan.

findings.title: W1 incorrectly narrows current traversal to regular files
findings.body: Current listing and reconciliation code only branches on `file_type().is_dir()` and processes every non-directory entry. Consequently, a symlink named `<key>.meta.json` is currently read through and can appear in listings or keep its referenced blob alive. W1 instead defines the exact yield set as regular files. An engineer implementing that contract with `is_file()` would hide such entries and could cause reconciliation to garbage-collect their blobs. The committed suite has no symlink characterization, so this behavior change would not be caught.
findings.recommendation: Before R-1, add stable-seam characterization tests for symlinked metadata in both `Store::list` and `reconcile::run_once`; then define W1 as the observed non-directory behavior, or route intentional narrowing through a behavior-changing pipeline.

findings.title: The committed lock never observes the temporary path R-2 changes
findings.body: The plan claims the golden test directly pins B3, including `.objects/.tmp-*`, but that test waits for `put_stream` to finish before inspecting the tree. Existing tests either assert that no `.tmp-` residue remains or manually create a `.tmp-stream`; none observe the actual writer-generated name during an active upload. `temp_blob_path` could therefore use a different prefix while all locked tests remain green, leaving interrupted uploads unrecognized and permanently ignored by reconciliation. A new R-1 unit test would encode the proposed implementation, not characterize the baseline through a surviving seam.
findings.recommendation: Commit a baseline test that blocks `Store::put_stream` mid-stream, observes exactly one `.objects/.tmp-*` entry, verifies reconciliation preserves it within grace, and then verifies cancellation/error cleanup.

next_steps: Revise the plan and strengthen the committed characterization baseline before starting R-1.
